Finterion Marketplace

Monetize your trading strategy. Publish your algorithm on Finterion and let investors subscribe to it.

Get Started →
📈

Prove Performance

Use this framework to backtest your strategy across multiple windows and build a verified track record.

🌐

Reach Investors

List your algorithm on the Finterion marketplace where investors browse and subscribe to strategies.

💰

Monetize

Set your own usage terms. Investors subscribe to your algorithm and you earn on your own conditions.

How to Publish Your Strategy
1

Install the Plugin

pip install finterion-investing-algorithm-framework
2

Get Your API Key

Sign up at finterion.com and grab the API key from your developer dashboard.

3

Register Components

Add the Finterion order executor, portfolio provider, and ping action to your algorithm.

4

Deploy & Monetize

Deploy your algorithm, set your usage terms, and start earning when investors subscribe.

Ready to monetize your trading algorithm?

Visit Finterion Marketplace →

Analysis Notes
🤖 AI Analysis
Export all strategy data, metrics, per-window breakdowns, and trade data as structured text. Paste into GitHub Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, or Claude for AI-powered analysis, strategy selection, and recommendations.
Tip: Use with the MCP server for deeper integration — Copilot can then query your backtest data directly in VS Code. pip install investing-algorithm-framework[mcp]
⚙ MCP Server Setup
The MCP server exposes all backtest data as tools an LLM can call. Start it from a terminal pointing at your backtest directory:
# Single directory iaf mcp -d /path/to/your/batch # Multiple directories iaf mcp -d batch_one -d batch_two # Or without the CLI installed python -m investing_algorithm_framework.cli.mcp_server -d /path/to/batch
Then configure VS Code (.vscode/settings.json):
{ "mcp": { "servers": { "backtest-analysis": { "command": "iaf", "args": ["mcp", "-d", "batch_one", "-d", "batch_two"] } } } }
Once running, Copilot Chat (or any MCP client) can list strategies, compare metrics, pull equity curves, create notes, and more — 23 tools in total.
